reforms.description: The U.S. is exploring regulatory experimentation for new financial technology initiatives, which may enhance access to credit for traditionally underserved groups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to credit can stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently in the exploratory phase.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is exploratory and does not represent a significant policy change.
reforms.source_quote: Regulators will need to find ways to ensure a level playing field between traditional financial institutions and the new entrants.

reforms.description: The tax reforms introduced in late 2017 significantly reduced marginal statutory tax rates for personal and corporate income, aiming to simplify the tax system and reduce embedded distortions, which is expected to spur greater investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the tax system and reducing distortions can encourage investment and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax reforms were enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: The main thrust of the reforms reduced marginal statutory tax rates for personal and corporate income, which combined with simplification will reduce distortions embedded in the tax system and spur greater investment.

reforms.description: Plans have been announced to boost public investment spending in areas such as infrastructure, skills, innovation, health, and environmental protection.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment in innovation and skills can enhance economic growth and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans have been publicly announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is part of broader investment plans rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Plans have been announced to boost public investment spending with long-term benefits: infrastructure, skills, innovation, health and environmental protection.

reforms.description: The establishment of a national innovation office aims to enhance coherence and continuity in the implementation of the national innovation strategy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A coherent national innovation strategy can streamline efforts and improve outcomes in innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The national innovation office has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a national office represents a significant structural change in innovation governance.
reforms.source_quote: The President has signed a Presidential Memorandum establishing a White House Office on American Innovation.

reforms.description: The Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2014 introduced reforms to assist job seekers by better matching them to growing sectors, aiming to build regional partnerships among employers, education and training providers, and public stakeholders.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving workforce skills and matching them to industry needs can enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The act has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This act represents a significant reform in workforce development and skills matching.
reforms.source_quote: The Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2014 introduced reforms to assisting job seekers by better matching them to growing sectors.

reforms.description: The tax reform enacted in late-2017 is expected to put government-sponsored enterprises (GSEs) under financial pressure due to a reduction in the corporate tax rate, which will trigger a large write-down on their tax-deferred assets. The reform aims to normalize housing finance by recommending the privatization of GSEs and subjecting them to the same regulations as other mortgage-backed securities issuers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the reform aims to normalize housing finance, it may create financial pressures that could hinder investment in GSEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been publicly announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the regulatory framework for GSEs.
reforms.source_quote: The last Economic Survey recommended leaving the securitisation of mortgages to the private sector.

reforms.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) allocated $2 billion in 2018 to expand broadband access to nearly 1 million homes, particularly in rural areas, to enhance connectivity and economic opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding broadband access can enhance economic opportunities and support innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The allocation has been made and the initiative is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is part of broader infrastructure investment efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has allocated $2 billion in 2018 to use in competitive bidding auctions to expand access to nearly 1 million homes.
